diagrams.title: Types of Nested Classes Hierarchy
diagrams.description: Shows the four types of nested classes in Java and their relationships
elements: Static Nested Class (inside, connected to class level)
Member Inner Class (inside, solid line to instance)
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the complete taxonomy of nested classes and understand where each type belongs in code structure

diagrams.title: Static vs Non-Static Inner Class Instantiation
diagrams.description: Shows how instantiation differs between static and non-static nested classes
elements: StaticNestedClass with dotted line to Outer (no instance needed)
InnerClass with solid line to specific Outer instance
Syntax annotations: Outer.StaticNested() vs outer.new InnerClass()
diagrams.purpose: Clarify the fundamental difference in how instances are created and their relationship to outer class instances

diagrams.title: Scope and Access Diagram for Local Inner Class
diagrams.description: Shows variable access rules for local inner classes within a method
elements: Local Inner Class box inside method
Outer class instance accessible from anywhere inside method
Arrows showing what can/cannot be accessed
diagrams.purpose: Understand access restrictions for local inner classes to avoid compilation errors

diagrams.title: Shadowing Demonstration
diagrams.description: Visual explanation of variable shadowing in nested classes
elements: Scope labels showing which x is used in each context
diagrams.purpose: Understand how to resolve naming conflicts when inner and outer classes have members with same name
